title: Rial Slides Past 1,400,000/USD
detail: The Iranian rial breached 1,400,000 per US dollar on the open market, a new record low. The Central Bank of Iran spent an estimated $800 million in reserves over the prior week attempting to defend the currency, but traders reported persistent sell pressure driven by sanctions enforcement and capital flight.

media.caption: Iranian rial slides past 1,400,000 per US dollar as Central Bank interventions fail

title: Gold Coin Prices Hit Historic High in Tehran
detail: The Bahar Azadi gold coin reached 92 million toman in Tehran markets, up 35% in three months. Iranians increasingly converted savings to gold and cryptocurrency as confidence in the rial collapsed. Queues formed outside exchange shops in central Tehran.

media.caption: Gold coin prices hit historic highs as Iranians convert savings amid rial collapse

title: Parliament Summons Central Bank Governor
detail: The Iranian Majlis Economic Committee summoned Central Bank Governor Mohammad Reza Farzin to explain the currency freefall. Hardline MPs accused the bank of mismanagement, while reformist factions blamed the broader impact of intensified US sanctions on Iran's oil exports.

media.caption: Iranian parliament summons Central Bank governor to explain currency freefall
